results.name: [admin] the temporary password opens only /change-password
[admin] rotating the password revokes the session and returns to /login
[admin] the old temporary password no longer works
[admin] the rotated password signs in and clears the gate
[admin] the app is holding a bearer token
[admin] RIASEC + SCCT install through the real builder service (D13, idempotent)
[admin] creates a college
[admin] adds a program under the college
[admin] the new program is listed on the college
[admin] creates a career
[admin] a lowercase RIASEC code is normalised to uppercase (iec → IEC)
[admin] maps the career to the program
[admin] the mapped career appears on the program
[counselor] the temporary password opens only /change-password
[counselor] rotating the password revokes the session and returns to /login
[counselor] the old temporary password no longer works
[counselor] the rotated password signs in and clears the gate
[counselor] class created; the join code is rendered
[counselor] the join code excludes the ambiguous alphabet (I, O, 0, 1)
[counselor] preview: duplicate suffixed, accents folded, mononym kept
[counselor] preview: the mononym has no last name (NULL, not "")
[counselor] preview persists nothing — it is the counselor who decides
[counselor] confirm creates the accounts in one batch
[counselor] the roster shows the edited username, not the proposed one
[counselor] the picker offers RIASEC because it has a PUBLISHED version
[counselor] assigns RIASEC to the class
[student] a wrong class code returns the generic 401
results.detail: 401 "The class code or username is incorrect."

results.name: [student] joins with only a class code and a username
[student] the join response leaks neither join_code nor counselor_id
[student] the student UI never renders the class join code
[student] GET /student/assignments answers 200
[student] the dashboard does NOT show "Nothing to do yet" (D11 — a 404 looks identical)
[student] GET /student/profile answers 200 and reports what §27 still needs
[student] POST .../start answers 200
[student] the player payload carries NO option score
[student] the player payload carries NO dimension
[student] the player payload has 60 questions
[student] section_label IS sent (the deliberate, limited disclosure)
[student] answered all 60 items through the UI
[student] every answer was POSTed and accepted
[student] the UI reports all 60 answered and enables submit
[student] POST .../submit scores INLINE — the result is in the same response
[student] the Holland Code is "IAR", as hand-computed before the run
[student] Investigative = 100.00 / "High Interest"
[student] all 6 dimensions scored (every one was answered)
[student] the result screen renders the Holland Code
[student] the result screen renders the per-dimension breakdown
[student] GET /student/recommendations answers 200 with data:null after RIASEC alone
[student] the screen says "finish both assessments" — not an empty list (§11 v1.2)
[counselor] assigns SCCT after RIASEC is complete — §27 needs both instruments
[student] starts SCCT (30 items)
[student] answered all 30 SCCT items
[student] SCCT submits and scores
[student] recommendations EXIST the moment the second instrument lands
[student] ranked careers and programs are both present (§27 ranks two things, separately)
[student] each type is ranked from 1, and at most 10 are kept (§27)
[student] scores descend — "ranking" means what it says
[student] every card carries a deterministic reason naming their top dimension
[student] a program recommendation resolves its college (§13.6 — a join, not a stored match)
[student] the recommendations screen renders a match percentage
[student] the screen states that no AI decided any of it (§3)
[admin] the browser extracts the PDF text and uploads both (§33)
[admin] the document reaches COMPLETED ("Ready")
[student] "Explain more" renders an AI paragraph grounded in the uploaded guide (§30)
[student] the deterministic §27 reason stays on the card — the AI elaborates, never replaces (§29)
[student] a second "Explain more" returns the stored explanation, not a regeneration (§20)

assessment.title: RIASEC Interest Inventory
assessment.instructions: Rate how much each statement sounds like you. There are no right or wrong answers — answer honestly rather than how you think you should.

questions.question_text: I enjoy working with tools, machines, or equipment.
I like building or repairing things with my hands.
I would rather work outdoors than in an office.
I am good at operating mechanical or electrical equipment.
I enjoy physical work that keeps me active.
I like figuring out how machines and devices work.
I would enjoy a job that involves driving or operating vehicles.
I prefer practical tasks with a visible result over abstract discussion.
I enjoy working with plants, animals, or the land.
I like assembling things from a set of parts and instructions.
I enjoy solving complex problems and puzzles.
I like conducting experiments to test an idea.
I enjoy analyzing data to find patterns.
I am curious about why things happen the way they do.
I like reading about scientific discoveries.
I enjoy working through a difficult mathematical problem.
I prefer to understand a theory thoroughly before applying it.
I like researching a topic in depth before forming an opinion.
I enjoy figuring out the cause of a problem no one else can explain.
I would enjoy working in a laboratory.
I enjoy expressing myself through art, music, or writing.
I like coming up with original ideas.
I would enjoy designing something no one has made before.
I prefer work that lets me be imaginative rather than follow a routine.
I enjoy performing, acting, or presenting to an audience.
I like taking photographs, drawing, or making videos.
I enjoy writing stories, poems, or essays.
I appreciate good design and notice when something is well made.
I would rather invent a new approach than follow an existing one.
I enjoy playing or composing music.
I enjoy helping other people with their problems.
I like teaching or explaining things to others.
I find it easy to understand how other people feel.
I enjoy working as part of a team.
I would find it rewarding to care for people who are unwell.
I like volunteering for community activities.
People often come to me for advice.
I enjoy meeting new people and making them feel welcome.
I would enjoy a job where I help others learn and grow.
I prefer cooperating with others over competing against them.
I enjoy persuading other people to see things my way.
I like taking the lead on group projects.
I would enjoy starting my own business.
I am comfortable making decisions that affect other people.
I enjoy selling ideas, products, or services.
I like setting ambitious goals and working to reach them.
I am comfortable speaking in front of a group.
I enjoy negotiating to get a good outcome.
I would enjoy managing a team of people.
I am willing to take risks to achieve something worthwhile.
I enjoy organizing information so it is easy to find.
I like following a clear set of procedures.
I am careful and accurate with details.
I enjoy keeping records and tracking numbers.
I prefer work where the expectations are clearly defined.
I like planning my tasks in advance and working to a schedule.
I enjoy working with spreadsheets or databases.
I notice small errors that other people miss.
I would enjoy managing budgets or accounts.
I prefer a well-ordered workplace with established routines.

assessment.title: SCCT Career Confidence Scale
assessment.instructions: Rate how strongly you agree with each statement about your confidence, expectations, and goals.

questions.question_text: I am confident I can succeed in the college program I choose.
I believe I can master difficult subjects if I put in the effort.
I can usually work out a solution when I face an academic setback.
I am confident I can meet the demands of a professional career.
I believe I have the skills needed to do well in my chosen field.
I can keep working on a task even when it becomes difficult.
I am confident I could learn a new skill my career required.
I trust my ability to make good decisions about my future.
I believe I can perform well under pressure.
I am confident I can complete a degree even if it takes hard work.
I believe working hard in school will lead to a good career.
I expect the program I choose will lead to real job opportunities.
I believe my education will improve my quality of life.
I expect that doing well now will open doors for me later.
I believe the effort I put into my studies will be rewarded.
I expect to be able to support myself through the career I choose.
I believe a career in my field of interest is respected by others.
I expect my work will make a positive difference to other people.
I believe the skills I am learning will still be valuable in the future.
I expect to find my future work satisfying.
I have a clear idea of the career I want to pursue.
I have set specific goals for my education.
I actively look for information about careers that interest me.
I am willing to plan several years ahead to reach my goals.
I intend to enroll in a college program related to my interests.
I regularly think about what I want to achieve in my career.
I am prepared to work toward a goal even without immediate reward.
I have taken concrete steps toward my future career.
I am committed to finishing what I start.
I intend to keep developing my skills after I finish school.

explanation.explanation_text:
Here's the explanation for the recommendation:

You have a strong match for a career as an Architect, with a match score of 70.6%. This is because your Investigative interest score is 100%, which aligns well with the Investigative dimension of the Holland RIASEC model that is typical of Architects. Additionally, your high SCCT career confidence score of 80% also suggests that you're a good fit for this career. According to the knowledge context, Architects use systematic problem-solving skills to examine a problem from multiple angles, and your interests in Investigative and Artistic dimensions, with a hint of Realistic, make you a good candidate for this type of work. However, it's essential to remember that this is just a guidance recommendation, and you should discuss it with your counselor and family to get a more comprehensive understanding of your options.

explanation.explanation_text:
Here's the explanation for the recommendation:

You have a strong match for a career as an Architect, with a match score of 70.6%. This is because your Investigative interest score is 100%, which aligns well with the Investigative dimension of the Holland RIASEC model that is typical of Architects. Additionally, your high SCCT career confidence score of 80% also suggests that you're a good fit for this career. According to the knowledge context, Architects use systematic problem-solving skills to examine a problem from multiple angles, and your interests in Investigative and Artistic dimensions, with a hint of Realistic, make you a good candidate for this type of work. However, it's essential to remember that this is just a guidance recommendation, and you should discuss it with your counselor and family to get a more comprehensive understanding of your options.
